dashboard.description: 6-layer trace: Cloudflare CDN → Palo Alto FW → F5 LTM → F5 Virtual Server → F5 Pool/Nodes → Angular Portal IIS Fleet. Layer 6 includes Prometheus metrics (PP01-PP07) + NR IIS log deep-dive (32 panels: error analysis + response time). v2: SRE annotations (BUSINESS/CALC/THRESHOLDS) + TIMESERIES fix.

panels.title: 🔗 INFRASTRUCTURE HEALTH — PA ↔ F5 ↔ Angular Portal

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: Palo Alto FW01 HA state (should be ACTIVE)

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: PAN: HA State

📐 CALC: Zabbix item 'PAN: HA State' from prdnextgenfirewall01 (Azure Production). queryType=2 (text). Value mapping: active=Green, passive=Blue

🔧 TECHNICAL: String metric: 'active' or 'passive'. Green=ACTIVE (healthy, processing traffic). Blue=PASSIVE (standby, FW02 is active). Both passive = CRITICAL: no firewall processing traffic

📊 BUSINESS: Confirms FW01 is the ACTIVE node in the Cloudflare -> PA -> F5 traffic path. If passive, FW02 has taken over -- check for failover cause immediately
panels.title: PA FW01 HA

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: Palo Alto FW02 HA state (should be PASSIVE/standby)

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: PAN: HA State

📐 CALC: Zabbix item 'PAN: HA State' from prdnextgenfirewall02. Same value mapping as FW01

🔧 TECHNICAL: String metric: 'active' or 'passive'. Blue=PASSIVE (normal standby). Green=ACTIVE (failover occurred). Both active = split-brain CRITICAL

📊 BUSINESS: FW02 should be PASSIVE (standby). If active, it means FW01 failed over -- investigate FW01 health. Both active = split-brain, escalate immediately
panels.title: PA FW02 HA

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: F5 LTM01 HA failover status (should be ACTIVE)

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: Failover status

📐 CALC: Zabbix item 'Failover status' from PRD-NG-LTM01.com. queryType=0 (numeric). Value mapping: 4=ACTIVE(green), 3=STANDBY(blue)

🔧 TECHNICAL: Numeric metric (3=standby, 4=active). Green=ACTIVE (healthy). If standby, LTM02 has taken over

📊 BUSINESS: Primary load balancer distributing traffic to Angular Portal IIS fleet. If standby, investigate failover cause -- LTM02 should have taken over
panels.title: F5 LTM01 HA

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: F5 LTM02 HA failover status (should be STANDBY)

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: Failover status

📐 CALC: Zabbix item 'Failover status' from PRD-NG-LTM02.com. Same mapping as LTM01

🔧 TECHNICAL: Numeric metric (3=standby, 4=active). Blue=STANDBY (normal). Active = failover occurred, investigate LTM01

📊 BUSINESS: Secondary load balancer, takes over if LTM01 fails. If active, LTM01 failed -- check LTM01 health and failover logs
panels.title: F5 LTM02 HA

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: Palo Alto active session count (concurrent connections through FW)

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: PAN: Active Sessions

📐 CALC: Zabbix item 'PAN: Active Sessions' from prdnextgenfirewall01. Last value. Unit: short

🔧 TECHNICAL: Session count. Green <500K (normal), Yellow 500K-5M (elevated, monitor), Red >5M (capacity risk, FW may start dropping sessions). Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: Total concurrent connections through the firewall. Capacity indicator for the entire Angular Portal traffic path. Sudden drop = possible FW issue
panels.title: PA Sessions

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: Palo Alto data plane CPU utilization (packet processing capacity)

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: PAN: Data Plane CPU Utilization

📐 CALC: Zabbix item 'PAN: Data Plane CPU Utilization' from prdnextgenfirewall01. Gauge visualization, 0-100%

🔧 TECHNICAL: Percentage metric. Green <60%, Yellow 60-80%, Red >80%. Data plane handles packet inspection, SSL, threat prevention. Unit: percent

📊 BUSINESS: High CPU = firewall becomes a bottleneck in the traffic path. >80% sustained = risk of packet drops and increased latency for all Angular Portal traffic
panels.title: PA CPU

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: F5 Virtual Server ANGULAR_iNMANPR_PP_443 active connections

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: Virtual server [/Common/ANGULAR_iNMANPR_PP_443]: Current connections

📐 CALC: Zabbix item 'Virtual server [/Common/ANGULAR_iNMANPR_PP_443]: Current connections'. Last value. Unit: short

🔧 TECHNICAL: Connection count. Green <5K, Yellow 5K-10K, Red >10K. Concurrent HTTPS sessions to the Angular Portal VS. Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: Primary load indicator for the Angular Portal virtual server. Correlate with F5 pool connections and backend node distribution
panels.title: Angular VS Conns

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: Average CPU across all Angular Portal IIS nodes (fleet-wide health)

📡 DATASOURCE: Prometheus (prometheus) | expr: avg(100 - avg by instance(rate(windows_cpu_time_total[idle])))

📐 CALC: avg(100 - (avg by (instance) (rate(windows_cpu_time_total{job="angular-prd-servers", mode="idle"}[$__rate_interval])) * 100)). Single number fleet average

🔧 TECHNICAL: Percentage metric. Green <60%, Yellow 60-80%, Red >80%. Inverted idle CPU rate across PP01-PP06 nodes. Unit: percent

📊 BUSINESS: Fleet-wide compute health indicator. Single number for quick triage. If high, drill into per-node CPU (CPU Usage by Node panel) to identify hot servers
panels.title: App Fleet CPU

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: Cloudflare CDN request volume over time (Layer 1 traffic)

📡 DATASOURCE: Cloudflare/Infinity (afctzdnavd1j4c) | CF GraphQL httpRequests1mGroups.requests

📐 CALC: Cloudflare GraphQL API: httpRequests1mGroups -> sum.requests. 1-minute granularity timeseries. Zone: Angular Portal

🔧 TECHNICAL: Request count per minute. No fixed thresholds (informational). Sudden drops = possible upstream issue. Spikes = traffic surge or attack

📊 BUSINESS: Layer 1 of the 6-layer trace: first point of contact for all Angular Portal users. Traffic entering the platform edge before any filtering

url_options.data: {"query":"{ viewer { zones(filter: { zoneTag: \"${cf_zone}\" }) { httpRequests1mGroups(limit: 1000, orderBy: [datetimeMinute_ASC], filter: { datetimeMinute_geq: \"${__from:date:iso}\", datetimeMinute_lt: \"${__to:date:iso}\" }) { sum { requests } dimensions { datetimeMinute } } } } }"}

panels.title: CF Requests Over Time

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: Total Cloudflare request count (aggregate CDN volume)

📡 DATASOURCE: Cloudflare/Infinity (afctzdnavd1j4c) | CF GraphQL httpRequests1mGroups.sum.requests

📐 CALC: CF GraphQL SUM of httpRequests1mGroups.requests over selected time window

🔧 TECHNICAL: Aggregate count. Purple background (informational). Represents all traffic before firewall filtering. Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: Total request volume at CDN edge. Baseline for calculating drop rates through subsequent layers (PA -> F5 -> IIS)

url_options.data: {"query":"{ viewer { zones(filter: { zoneTag: \"${cf_zone}\" }) { httpRequests1mGroups(limit: 1000, filter: { datetimeMinute_geq: \"${__from:date:iso}\", datetimeMinute_lt: \"${__to:date:iso}\" }) { sum { requests } } } } }"}

panels.title: CF Requests

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: Cloudflare threat/block count (WAF + bot detections)

📡 DATASOURCE: Cloudflare/Infinity (afctzdnavd1j4c) | CF GraphQL httpRequests1mGroups.sum.threats

📐 CALC: CF GraphQL threats metric from httpRequests1mGroups. Total blocked/challenged requests

🔧 TECHNICAL: Threat count. Non-zero is expected (bots, scanners). Green <100, Yellow 100-1K, Red >1K (active attack). Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: WAF/firewall rule blocks and bot detections. Spikes indicate active attacks. Compare with CF Requests to assess attack-to-traffic ratio

url_options.data: {"query":"{ viewer { zones(filter: { zoneTag: \"${cf_zone}\" }) { httpRequests1mGroups(limit: 1000, filter: { datetimeMinute_geq: \"${__from:date:iso}\", datetimeMinute_lt: \"${__to:date:iso}\" }) { sum { threats } } } } }"}

panels.title: CF Threats

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: Cloudflare bandwidth over time (data transfer at CDN edge)

📡 DATASOURCE: Cloudflare/Infinity (afctzdnavd1j4c) | CF GraphQL httpRequests1mGroups.bytes

📐 CALC: CF GraphQL bytes metric from httpRequests1mGroups. Total bytes transferred per time bucket

🔧 TECHNICAL: Bytes metric, displayed in decbytes. No fixed thresholds (informational). Shows payload sizes and caching effectiveness trends. Unit: decbytes

📊 BUSINESS: Data transfer volume at CDN edge. Correlate with request count to derive average payload size. Spikes without request increase = large file downloads

url_options.data: {"query":"{ viewer { zones(filter: { zoneTag: \"${cf_zone}\" }) { httpRequests1mGroups(limit: 1000, orderBy: [datetimeMinute_ASC], filter: { datetimeMinute_geq: \"${__from:date:iso}\", datetimeMinute_lt: \"${__to:date:iso}\" }) { sum { bytes cachedBytes } dimensions { datetimeMinute } } } } }"}

panels.title: CF Bandwidth Over Time

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: Total Cloudflare bandwidth (aggregate bytes at CDN edge)

📡 DATASOURCE: Cloudflare/Infinity (afctzdnavd1j4c) | CF GraphQL httpRequests1mGroups.sum.bytes

📐 CALC: CF GraphQL SUM of bytes from httpRequests1mGroups over selected time window

🔧 TECHNICAL: Aggregate bytes. Purple background (informational). Unit: decbytes

📊 BUSINESS: Total data transfer at CDN edge. Capacity and cost indicator. Compare with origin bandwidth to measure cache offload effectiveness

url_options.data: {"query":"{ viewer { zones(filter: { zoneTag: \"${cf_zone}\" }) { httpRequests1mGroups(limit: 1000, filter: { datetimeMinute_geq: \"${__from:date:iso}\", datetimeMinute_lt: \"${__to:date:iso}\" }) { sum { bytes } } } } }"}

panels.title: CF Bandwidth

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: Cloudflare cache hit ratio (% served from edge without origin)

📡 DATASOURCE: Cloudflare/Infinity (afctzdnavd1j4c) | CF GraphQL cachedRequests / requests

📐 CALC: CF GraphQL cachedRequests / total requests * 100. Percentage of requests served from CF edge cache

🔧 TECHNICAL: Percentage metric. Green >80% (good caching), Yellow 50-80% (review cache rules), Red <50% (cache miss heavy). Unit: percentunit

📊 BUSINESS: Higher cache ratio = better performance and lower origin load. Drop in cache % = possible cache purge, config change, or new uncacheable content

url_options.data: {"query":"{ viewer { zones(filter: { zoneTag: \"${cf_zone}\" }) { httpRequests1mGroups(limit: 1000, filter: { datetimeMinute_geq: \"${__from:date:iso}\", datetimeMinute_lt: \"${__to:date:iso}\" }) { sum { requests cachedRequests } } } } }"}

panels.title: CF Cache %

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: PA eth1/1 (External) traffic rate -- Cloudflare-facing interface

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: Interface ethernet1/1(EXT): Bits received | Bits sent

📐 CALC: Two Zabbix items: 'Interface ethernet1/1(EXT): Bits received' and 'Bits sent' from prdnextgenfirewall01. Unit: bps

🔧 TECHNICAL: Bits per second, inbound and outbound. All inbound traffic enters here from Cloudflare CDN edge. Unit: bps

📊 BUSINESS: Total platform ingress from CDN edge. First metric in the CF -> PA path. Sudden drop = CF routing issue. Asymmetry = normal (responses > requests)
panels.title: eth1/1(EXT) — Cloudflare Facing

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: PA eth1/2 (Internal) traffic rate -- F5 LTM-facing interface

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: Interface ethernet1/2(INT): Bits received | Bits sent

📐 CALC: Two Zabbix items: 'Interface ethernet1/2(INT): Bits received' and 'Bits sent'. Traffic after firewall inspection, heading to F5 LTM. Unit: bps

🔧 TECHNICAL: Bits per second. Should closely track eth1/1. Delta between eth1/1 and eth1/2 = FW blocked traffic. Unit: bps

📊 BUSINESS: Traffic exiting PA toward F5 after inspection. Compare with eth1/1 to measure FW drop rate. Large gap = firewall blocking significant traffic
panels.title: eth1/2(INT) — F5 LTM Facing

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: PA eth1/3 (DMZ) interface traffic rate

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: Interface ethernet1/3(DMZ): Bits received | Bits sent

📐 CALC: Two Zabbix items: 'Interface ethernet1/3(DMZ): Bits received' and 'Bits sent'. Unit: bps

🔧 TECHNICAL: Bits per second. Typically lower volume than EXT/INT interfaces. Used for management or secondary services. Unit: bps

📊 BUSINESS: DMZ interface traffic. Normally low. Unexpected spikes = investigate unauthorized traffic or misconfigured routes
panels.title: eth1/3(DMZ)

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: PA session breakdown by protocol (Total/TCP/UDP/ICMP)

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: PAN: Active Sessions | TCP | UDP | ICMP

📐 CALC: Four Zabbix items: PAN Active Sessions, Active TCP Sessions, Active UDP Sessions, Active ICMP Sessions. Timeseries overlay

🔧 TECHNICAL: Session counts by protocol. TCP dominates for HTTPS traffic. UDP spike = possible DNS flood or VPN traffic. Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: Protocol distribution reveals attack patterns. UDP/ICMP spikes without TCP increase = possible DDoS. TCP-only growth = legitimate traffic increase
panels.title: PA Sessions (Total/TCP/UDP/ICMP)

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: PA CPU for Data Plane and Management Plane

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: PAN: Data Plane CPU | Management Plane CPU

📐 CALC: Two Zabbix items: 'PAN: Data Plane CPU Utilization' and 'PAN: Management Plane CPU Utilization'. Timeseries

🔧 TECHNICAL: Percentage 0-100%. Data plane: packet processing (critical). Mgmt plane: config/logging (less critical). Unit: percent

📊 BUSINESS: Data plane CPU directly impacts throughput. >80% = packet drops risk. Mgmt plane >80% = logging delays but no traffic impact
panels.title: PA CPU (Data + Mgmt Plane)

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: PA interface errors and packet drops across all ethernet interfaces

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: /Interface ethernet1\/.*: Inbound packets with errors/ | /discarded/

📐 CALC: Zabbix regex items: 'Interface ethernet1/*: Inbound packets with errors' and 'Inbound packets discarded'. All interfaces

🔧 TECHNICAL: Error/drop counts. Any non-zero = network issues. Should be zero in normal operation. Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: Any non-zero indicates network issues in the CF -> PA -> F5 path. Drops = traffic lost before reaching backend. Investigate NIC, cable, or capacity
panels.title: PA Interface Errors & Drops

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: F5 Interface 1.1 total data traffic (all VLANs)

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: Interface [1.1]: Incoming traffic, rate | Outgoing traffic, rate

📐 CALC: Two Zabbix items: 'Interface [1.1]: Incoming traffic, rate' and 'Outgoing traffic, rate' from both LTMs. Unit: Bps

🔧 TECHNICAL: Bytes per second. Single data interface carrying ALL traffic via VLANs. Total throughput capacity indicator. Unit: Bps

📊 BUSINESS: Total F5 throughput. Compare with PA eth1/2 to verify all traffic reaches the LB. Drop between PA and F5 = network issue in the path
panels.title: F5 Interface 1.1 — All Data Traffic

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: F5 client-side connections (PA/clients to F5)

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: HA: Client Current Connections

📐 CALC: Zabbix item 'HA: Client Current Connections' from both LTMs (regex filter). Timeseries

🔧 TECHNICAL: Connection count. Shows demand from external network on the load balancer. Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: Total concurrent connections from PA/clients to F5. Compare with server connections to measure connection multiplexing effectiveness
panels.title: F5 Client Connections

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: F5 server-side connections (F5 to backend IIS nodes)

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: HA: Server Current Connections

📐 CALC: Zabbix item 'HA: Server Current Connections' from both LTMs. Timeseries

🔧 TECHNICAL: Connection count. Shows actual load distributed to Angular Portal IIS fleet. Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: Connections from F5 to backend IIS nodes. Server < Client = connection multiplexing working. Server >> Client = backend connection leak
panels.title: F5 Server Connections

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: F5 new connection rate (HTTPS sessions per second)

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: HA: Client New Connections per sec

📐 CALC: Zabbix item 'HA: Client New Connections per sec' from both LTMs. Timeseries

🔧 TECHNICAL: Connections per second. Measures connection churn, not total connections. High rate = many short-lived sessions. Unit: cps

📊 BUSINESS: New HTTPS session establishment rate. Spike in new connections with stable total = high churn. Drop = traffic routing away or upstream issue

item.filter: HA: Client New Connections per sec

panels.title: F5 New Connections/sec

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: F5 TCP connection states (Open, CLOSE-WAIT/LAST-ACK, TIME-WAIT)

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: Open TCP connections | CLOSE-WAIT/LAST-ACK | TIME-WAIT

📐 CALC: Three Zabbix items from LTM01: Open TCP connections, TCP connections CLOSE-WAIT/LAST-ACK, TIME-WAIT. Timeseries

🔧 TECHNICAL: Connection state counts. TIME-WAIT buildup = connection churn. CLOSE-WAIT = backend not closing. Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: State distribution reveals backend health. High TIME-WAIT = many short connections. High CLOSE-WAIT = backend app not properly closing connections
panels.title: F5 TCP States

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: F5 memory utilization percentage

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: Memory: Utilization

📐 CALC: Zabbix item 'Memory: Utilization' from both LTMs (regex filter). Percentage 0-100%

🔧 TECHNICAL: Percentage metric. Green <70%, Yellow 70-85%, Red >85%. Affects connection table capacity and SSL session caching. Unit: percent

📊 BUSINESS: RAM usage on the load balancer. High memory = risk of connection table exhaustion and SSL session cache eviction, causing re-handshakes
panels.title: F5 Memory %

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: VS ANGULAR_iNMANPR_PP_443 active connections (concurrent HTTPS sessions)

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: Virtual server [/Common/ANGULAR_iNMANPR_PP_443]: Current connections

📐 CALC: Zabbix item 'Virtual server [/Common/ANGULAR_iNMANPR_PP_443]: Current connections'. Last value

🔧 TECHNICAL: Connection count. Green <5K, Yellow 5K-10K, Red >10K. Primary load metric for this VS. Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: Concurrent HTTPS sessions to Angular Portal VS. Primary load indicator. Correlate with pool connections to verify backend distribution
panels.title: VS Active Conns

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: VS usage ratio (5-minute average capacity utilization)

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: Virtual server [/Common/ANGULAR_iNMANPR_PP_443]: Usage ratio, avg 5m

📐 CALC: Zabbix item 'Virtual server [/Common/ANGULAR_iNMANPR_PP_443]: Usage ratio, avg 5m'. Gauge 0-100%

🔧 TECHNICAL: Percentage of VS connection capacity used. Green <50%, Yellow 50-80%, Red >80%. Gauge visualization. Unit: percent

📊 BUSINESS: Quick capacity assessment. >80% = VS approaching connection limit, may need capacity increase or traffic shedding
panels.title: VS Usage 5m

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: VS incoming traffic rate (client request payload bytes/sec)

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: Virtual server [/Common/ANGULAR_iNMANPR_PP_443]: Incoming traffic, rate

📐 CALC: Zabbix item 'Virtual server [/Common/ANGULAR_iNMANPR_PP_443]: Incoming traffic, rate'. Last value

🔧 TECHNICAL: Bytes per second of client requests. Measures request payload volume. Unit: Bps

📊 BUSINESS: Request payload volume arriving at Angular Portal VS. Compare with outgoing to measure request/response asymmetry
panels.title: VS In Rate

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: VS outgoing traffic rate (response payload bytes/sec)

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: Virtual server [/Common/ANGULAR_iNMANPR_PP_443]: Outgoing traffic, rate

📐 CALC: Zabbix item 'Virtual server [/Common/ANGULAR_iNMANPR_PP_443]: Outgoing traffic, rate'. Typically larger than incoming (responses > requests)

🔧 TECHNICAL: Bytes per second of responses. Typically > incoming rate. Unit: Bps

📊 BUSINESS: Response payload volume sent back through the VS. Out >> In is normal. Out dropping while In stable = backend errors returning small error pages
panels.title: VS Out Rate

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: VS rate limit hits (connections rejected by F5 rate limiting)

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: Virtual server [/Common/ANGULAR_iNMANPR_PP_443]: Connections hit a rate limit

📐 CALC: Zabbix item 'Virtual server [/Common/ANGULAR_iNMANPR_PP_443]: Connections hit a rate limit'. Last value

🔧 TECHNICAL: Count of rejected connections. Should be zero. Non-zero = traffic being throttled. Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: Any non-zero means traffic is being actively throttled by F5 rate limiting. Investigate whether rate limits need adjusting or if it is a legitimate attack

item.filter: Virtual server [/Common/ANGULAR_iNMANPR_PP_443]: Connections hit a rate limit

panels.title: VS Rate Limit Hits

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: VS packet rate in/out (packets per second through VS)

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: VS Incoming/Outgoing packet rate

📐 CALC: Two Zabbix items: Incoming/Outgoing packet rate for ANGULAR_iNMANPR_PP_443. Summed

🔧 TECHNICAL: Packets per second. Complements byte rate to show packet overhead and sizing. Unit: pps

📊 BUSINESS: Packet rate through the VS. Compare bytes/packets to derive average packet size. Small packets = overhead-heavy traffic
panels.title: VS Pkt In/Out

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: VS connections over time (timeline of concurrent HTTPS sessions)

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: Virtual server [/Common/ANGULAR_iNMANPR_PP_443]: Current connections

📐 CALC: Zabbix item 'Virtual server [.../ANGULAR_iNMANPR_PP_443]: Current connections'. Timeseries

🔧 TECHNICAL: Connection count over time. Shows traffic patterns, peaks, and anomalies. Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: Timeline view of VS concurrent connections. Shows daily patterns, peaks, and anomalies. Correlate spikes with incidents
panels.title: VS Connections Over Time

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: VS traffic in/out over time (bandwidth timeline)

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: VS Incoming/Outgoing traffic, rate

📐 CALC: Two Zabbix timeseries: Incoming and Outgoing traffic rate for ANGULAR_iNMANPR_PP_443

🔧 TECHNICAL: Bytes per second over time. Shows data transfer patterns and asymmetry trends. Unit: Bps

📊 BUSINESS: Bandwidth timeline through the VS. In/Out asymmetry is normal. Sudden convergence = backend returning errors (small responses)
panels.title: VS Traffic In/Out

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: VS usage ratio at 3 time scales (5s/1m/5m smoothing)

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: VS Usage ratio avg 5s | 1m | 5m

📐 CALC: Three Zabbix items: Usage ratio avg 5s, 1m, 5m for ANGULAR_iNMANPR_PP_443. Timeseries overlay

🔧 TECHNICAL: Percentage 0-100% at 3 smoothing levels. 5s catches micro-bursts, 5m shows sustained load. Unit: percent

📊 BUSINESS: Capacity utilization with different smoothing. If 5s spikes high but 5m is low = micro-bursts. If 5m is high = sustained capacity pressure
panels.title: VS Usage Ratio (5s/1m/5m)
🏊 LAYER 5 — F5 Pools & Backend Nodes (Angular + iCMANPR-PP)

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: Angular Pool availability status (F5 pool health)

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: Pool [/Common/ANGULAR_iNMANPR_PP.pool_80]: Status available

📐 CALC: Zabbix item 'Pool [/Common/ANGULAR_iNMANPR_PP.pool_80]: Status available'. Value mapping: 1=Available(green), 0=Down(red)

🔧 TECHNICAL: Binary status. Green=Available (pool has healthy members). Red=Down (all members failed). Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: Angular-specific IIS app pool (port 80). Routes Angular-only endpoints. Down = Angular Portal completely unavailable for these routes
panels.title: Angular Pool

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: Angular Pool active connections (concurrent connections to Angular backend)

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: Pool [/Common/ANGULAR_iNMANPR_PP.pool_80]: Current connections

📐 CALC: Zabbix item 'Pool [/Common/ANGULAR_iNMANPR_PP.pool_80]: Current connections'. Last value

🔧 TECHNICAL: Connection count. Lower traffic pool (Angular-only endpoints). Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: Concurrent connections distributed across Angular-specific backend nodes. Compare with Main PP to see relative traffic split
panels.title: Angular Pool Conns

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: Main PP Pool availability status (high-traffic F5 pool)

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: Pool [/Common/iCMANPR-PP.pool_80]: Status available

📐 CALC: Zabbix item 'Pool [/Common/iCMANPR-PP.pool_80]: Status available'. Value mapping: 1=Available(green), 0=Down(red)

🔧 TECHNICAL: Binary status. Green=Available, Red=Down. The high-traffic pool serving all brand traffic across PP01-PP12. Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: Main PP pool serves all brand traffic. Down = complete Angular Portal outage for multi-brand endpoints. Higher priority than Angular pool
panels.title: Main PP Pool

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: Main PP Pool active connections (multi-brand traffic distribution)

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: Pool [/Common/iCMANPR-PP.pool_80]: Current connections

📐 CALC: Zabbix item 'Pool [/Common/iCMANPR-PP.pool_80]: Current connections'. Last value

🔧 TECHNICAL: Connection count. Typically 10-100x higher than Angular Pool due to multi-brand traffic. Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: Concurrent connections across the high-traffic pool. Compare with Angular Pool to see traffic split. Spike = possible upstream issue or attack
panels.title: Main PP Conns

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: Angular Pool queue depth (connections waiting for available backend)

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: Pool [/Common/ANGULAR_iNMANPR_PP.pool_80]: Queue

📐 CALC: Zabbix item 'Pool [/Common/ANGULAR_iNMANPR_PP.pool_80]: Queue'. Last value

🔧 TECHNICAL: Queue count. Should be zero. Non-zero = ALL pool members at capacity. Red >=1. Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: Connections waiting for an available backend node. Non-zero means ALL pool members are at capacity -- immediate action needed to scale or investigate backend health
panels.title: Angular Queue

panels.description:

📐 CALC: Zabbix item 'Pool [/Common/iCMANPR-PP.pool_80]: Queue'. Last value

🔧 TECHNICAL: Queue count. Should be zero. Non-zero = ALL pool members at capacity. Impacts ALL brands. Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: Queuing here impacts ALL brands on the Angular Portal platform. Non-zero = immediate escalation. Check backend node health and pool member count
panels.title: Main PP Queue

panels.description:

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: Angular pool connections | Main PP pool connections

📐 CALC: Two Zabbix timeseries: Angular pool and Main PP pool current connections. Overlay comparison

🔧 TECHNICAL: Connection counts over time. Shows relative load distribution between pool types. Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: Relative load distribution between the two pool types. Main PP should always be higher. Angular matching Main PP = possible routing misconfiguration
panels.title: Pool Connections — Angular vs Main PP

panels.description:

📐 CALC: Two Zabbix timeseries: Outgoing traffic rate for both pools. Unit: Bps

🔧 TECHNICAL: Bytes per second outgoing from each pool. Shows response payload distribution. Unit: Bps

📊 BUSINESS: Data served by Angular vs Main PP pools. Response payload distribution between pool types. Use to size backend capacity per pool
panels.title: Pool Outgoing Traffic — Angular vs Main PP

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: Per-node backend connection distribution (PP01-PP12)

📡 DATASOURCE: Zabbix (cfcwccg0qvmyof) | item: /Node [/Common/iCMANPR-PP.*]: Current connections/

📐 CALC: Zabbix regex item 'Node [/Common/iCMANPR-PP.*]: Current connections'. Per-node timeseries

🔧 TECHNICAL: Per-node connection counts. Validates load balancing effectiveness. Even distribution = healthy. Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: Shows how F5 distributes connections across PP01-PP12 nodes. Uneven distribution = possible node health issues or LB algorithm misconfiguration
panels.title: Backend Node Connections (PP01-PP12)

panels.description:

🔧 TECHNICAL: Binary: 1=Running(green), 0/<1=Down(red). Checks if at least one IIS app pool is Running on PP01. Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: PP01 node health. Red = all IIS app pools down on this server, requests will fail. Check IIS Manager and Event Viewer on PP01

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: IIS worker process failure count across the fleet (W3WP crashes)

📡 DATASOURCE: Prometheus (prometheus) | expr: sum(windows_iis_total_worker_process_failures{job="angular-prd-servers"})

📐 CALC: sum(windows_iis_total_worker_process_failures{job="angular-prd-servers"}). Counter metric, shows cumulative crashes

🔧 TECHNICAL: Cumulative crash count. Green 0 (healthy), Yellow >=1 (investigate), Red >=5 (critical, multiple pools crashing). Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: Each failure = app pool recycling, user sessions lost, requests failed. Rising count = application instability. Check Windows Event Viewer for crash details
panels.title: W.Failures

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: 4xx client errors in last hour (404 + 423 across IIS fleet)

📡 DATASOURCE: Prometheus (prometheus) | expr: sum(increase(windows_iis_not_found_errors_total[1h])) + sum(increase(locked_errors[1h]))

📐 CALC: sum(increase(windows_iis_not_found_errors_total{site=~"$app"}[1h])) + sum(increase(windows_iis_locked_errors_total{site=~"$app"}[1h])). Filtered by $app template variable

🔧 TECHNICAL: Error count over 1h window. Green <50, Yellow 50-200, Red >200. Includes 404 Not Found + 423 Locked. Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: Client errors filtered by selected IIS site ($app). Rising count = possible broken links, missing assets, or config issues. Check Error URLs table for details
panels.title: 4xx (1h)

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: 5xx server errors in last hour (worker request failures across fleet)

📡 DATASOURCE: Prometheus (prometheus) | expr: sum(increase(windows_iis_worker_request_errors_total[1h]))

📐 CALC: sum(increase(windows_iis_worker_request_errors_total{site=~"$app"}[1h])) OR vector(0). Filtered by $app

🔧 TECHNICAL: Error count over 1h window. Green 0, Yellow >=1, Red >=10. ANY non-zero needs investigation. Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: Server errors are user-facing failures. ANY non-zero needs investigation: 500=crash, 502=upstream timeout, 503=app pool down. Check 5xx Error Rate Trend for timeline
panels.title: 5xx (1h)

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: CPU usage per IIS node over time (per-server breakdown)

📡 DATASOURCE: Prometheus (prometheus) | expr: 100 - avg by(instance)(rate(windows_cpu_time_total{idle}))

📐 CALC: 100 - (avg by (instance) (rate(windows_cpu_time_total{job="angular-prd-servers", mode="idle"}[$__rate_interval])) * 100). Per-node timeseries

🔧 TECHNICAL: Percentage 0-100% per node. Inverted idle CPU rate. Identifies hot servers vs fleet-wide issues. Unit: percent

📊 BUSINESS: Essential for isolating server-specific issues vs fleet-wide problems. One hot node = possible stuck process. All hot = traffic surge or capacity issue
panels.title: CPU Usage by Node

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: Memory usage per IIS node over time (RAM consumption breakdown)

📡 DATASOURCE: Prometheus (prometheus) | expr: (1 - free_bytes/total_bytes) * 100 per instance

📐 CALC: (1 - sum by(instance)(windows_os_physical_memory_free_bytes) / sum by(instance)(windows_cs_physical_memory_bytes)) * 100

🔧 TECHNICAL: Percentage 0-100% per node. Green <80%, Yellow 80-90%, Red >90%. High memory = risk of app pool recycling. Unit: percent

📊 BUSINESS: Tracks RAM consumption across PP01-PP06. High memory = risk of app pool recycling and OOM failures. Memory leak = steadily rising line over hours
panels.title: Memory Usage by Node

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: Cluster request rate (RPS) -- THE KEY METRIC for Angular Portal throughput

📡 DATASOURCE: Prometheus (prometheus) | expr: sum(rate(windows_iis_requests_total)) | per-instance breakdown

📐 CALC: Two series: (A) sum(rate(windows_iis_requests_total{site=~"$app"}[$__rate_interval])) = total RPS, (B) sum by (instance)(rate(...)) = per-node RPS

🔧 TECHNICAL: Requests per second. The definitive throughput measure. Unit: reqps

📊 BUSINESS: THE KEY METRIC. Total and per-node request rate. Drop = traffic issue or upstream failure. Spike = attack or campaign. Per-node shows LB distribution
panels.title: Cluster Request Rate (RPS) — THE KEY METRIC

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: 4xx error rate trend over time (errors/minute: 404 + 423)

📡 DATASOURCE: Prometheus (prometheus) | expr: rate(not_found + locked errors) * 60

📐 CALC: Three series: (A) rate(not_found_errors)*60, (B) rate(locked_errors)*60, (C) combined total. Unit is errors/minute

🔧 TECHNICAL: Rate * 60 = errors per minute. Shows error patterns over time. Unit: short (errors/minute). Correlate spikes with deployments or config changes

📊 BUSINESS: Error rate trend helps correlate with deployments or config changes. Rising trend without traffic increase = application issue, not load-related
panels.title: 4xx Error Rate Trend

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: 5xx error rate trend over time (CRITICAL, errors/minute with per-node breakdown)

📡 DATASOURCE: Prometheus (prometheus) | expr: rate(worker_request_errors_total) * 60

📐 CALC: Two series: (A) sum(rate(worker_request_errors)*60 = total, (B) sum by instance = per-node. Unit is errors/minute

🔧 TECHNICAL: Rate * 60 = errors per minute. Any sustained non-zero rate requires immediate investigation. Unit: short (errors/minute)

📊 BUSINESS: CRITICAL metric. Any sustained non-zero rate = active server-side failures. Per-node view isolates whether one server or fleet-wide. Escalate if sustained
panels.title: 5xx Error Rate Trend (CRITICAL)

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: Data sent per node (stacked outgoing bandwidth from each IIS server)

📡 DATASOURCE: Prometheus (prometheus) | expr: sum by(instance)(rate(windows_iis_sent_bytes_total))

📐 CALC: sum by (instance)(rate(windows_iis_sent_bytes_total{site=~"$app"}[$__rate_interval])). Stacked visualization

🔧 TECHNICAL: Bytes per second per node. Stacked view shows total fleet egress and per-node contribution. Unit: Bps

📊 BUSINESS: Outgoing bandwidth from each IIS server. Stacked = total fleet egress. One node much higher = possible large file serving or imbalanced routing
panels.title: Data Sent by Node (Stacked)

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: Data received per node (stacked incoming bandwidth to each IIS server)

📡 DATASOURCE: Prometheus (prometheus) | expr: sum by(instance)(rate(windows_iis_received_bytes_total))

📐 CALC: sum by (instance)(rate(windows_iis_received_bytes_total{site=~"$app"}[$__rate_interval])). Stacked visualization

🔧 TECHNICAL: Bytes per second per node. Stacked view shows total fleet ingress and per-node contribution. Unit: Bps

📊 BUSINESS: Incoming bandwidth to each IIS server. Compare with sent bytes to see request/response asymmetry per node
panels.title: Data Received by Node (Stacked)

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: Total IIS request count from NR logs (all access log entries)

📡 DATASOURCE: NewRelic/Infinity (efbmrprl96ry8e) | NRQL: COUNT(*) FROM Log WHERE service='iis'

📐 CALC: COUNT(*) from NR Log WHERE service='iis' AND message NOT LIKE '%#%' (excludes W3C header comments). NR account 7463762

🔧 TECHNICAL: Aggregate count. Complement to Prometheus RPS, provides log-level detail. Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: Total requests from log perspective. Compare with Prometheus RPS to verify log pipeline completeness. Gap = logging issue or filter mismatch

url_options.data: {"query": "{ actor { account(id: 7463762) { nrql(query: \"SELECT count(*) AS 'Total' FROM Log WHERE service = 'iis' AND message NOT LIKE '%#%' SINCE ${__from:date:seconds} UNTIL ${__to:date:seconds}\") { results } } } }"}

panels.title: IIS Requests

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: Overall IIS error rate percentage (non-200/304 responses)

📡 DATASOURCE: NewRelic/Infinity (efbmrprl96ry8e) | NRQL: percentage(count(*), WHERE status NOT IN ('200','304'))

📐 CALC: percentage(count(*), WHERE capture(status) NOT IN ('200','304')). Uses regex capture for HTTP status from W3C log

🔧 TECHNICAL: Percentage metric. Green <1% (healthy), Yellow 1-5% (elevated), Red >5% (degraded). Unit: percent

📊 BUSINESS: Top-line health metric from the log perspective. >5% = widespread issues across the Angular Portal fleet. Check Error URLs table for root cause

url_options.data: {"query": "{ actor { account(id: 7463762) { nrql(query: \"SELECT percentage(count(*), WHERE message NOT LIKE '%200 0%' AND message NOT LIKE '%304 0%') AS 'Rate' FROM Log WHERE service = 'iis' AND message NOT LIKE '%#%' SINCE ${__from:date:seconds} UNTIL ${__to:date:seconds}\") { results } } } }"}

panels.title: Error Rate %

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: 4xx client error count from IIS logs (400-429 responses)

📡 DATASOURCE: NewRelic/Infinity (efbmrprl96ry8e) | NRQL: COUNT(*) WHERE capture(status) LIKE '4%'

📐 CALC: COUNT(*) WHERE capture(status) LIKE '4%' from NR Log. Regex extracts status code from W3C format

🔧 TECHNICAL: Error count. Green <100, Yellow 100-500, Red >500. Includes 400 Bad Request, 403 Forbidden, 404 Not Found, 408 Timeout. Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: Client-side errors. High count = broken links, missing assets, or client misconfiguration. Check Error URLs by Status Code table for breakdown

url_options.data: {"query": "{ actor { account(id: 7463762) { nrql(query: \"SELECT filter(count(*), WHERE message LIKE '% 400 %' OR message LIKE '% 401 %' OR message LIKE '% 403 %' OR message LIKE '% 404 %' OR message LIKE '% 405 %' OR message LIKE '% 429 %') AS 'Cnt' FROM Log WHERE service = 'iis' AND message NOT LIKE '%#%' SINCE ${__from:date:seconds} UNTIL ${__to:date:seconds}\") { results } } } }"}

panels.title: 4xx Errors

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: 5xx server error count from IIS logs (server-side failures)

📡 DATASOURCE: NewRelic/Infinity (efbmrprl96ry8e) | NRQL: COUNT(*) WHERE capture(status) LIKE '5%'

📐 CALC: COUNT(*) WHERE capture(status) LIKE '5%' from NR Log

🔧 TECHNICAL: Error count. Green 0, Yellow >=1, Red >=10. ANY non-zero is actionable: 500=crash, 502=upstream timeout, 503=app pool down. Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: Server-side errors are user-facing failures. ANY non-zero needs investigation. Check Error URLs and Recent IIS Error Logs for root cause

url_options.data: {"query": "{ actor { account(id: 7463762) { nrql(query: \"SELECT filter(count(*), WHERE message LIKE '% 500 %' OR message LIKE '% 502 %' OR message LIKE '% 503 %' OR message LIKE '% 504 %') AS 'Cnt' FROM Log WHERE service = 'iis' AND message NOT LIKE '%#%' SINCE ${__from:date:seconds} UNTIL ${__to:date:seconds}\") { results } } } }"}

panels.title: 5xx Errors

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: Slow request count (>5s response time, severe UX degradation)

📡 DATASOURCE: NewRelic/Infinity (efbmrprl96ry8e) | NRQL: COUNT(*) WHERE numeric(capture(time-taken)) > 5000

📐 CALC: COUNT(*) WHERE numeric(capture(time-taken)) > 5000 from NR Log. Extracts time-taken (ms) from W3C log

🔧 TECHNICAL: Count of requests >5s. Green <100, Yellow 100-500, Red >500. Severe user experience degradation threshold. Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: Requests taking >5s represent severe UX degradation. Check Slowest URLs table to identify which endpoints are slow and optimize

url_options.data: {"query": "{ actor { account(id: 7463762) { nrql(query: \"SELECT count(*) AS 'Slow' FROM Log WHERE service = 'iis' AND message NOT LIKE '%#%' AND numeric(capture(message, r'.* [0-9][0-9][0-9] [0-9]+ [0-9]+ (?P<ms>[0-9]+) .*')) > 5000 SINCE ${__from:date:seconds} UNTIL ${__to:date:seconds}\") { results } } } }"}

panels.title: Slow (>5s)

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: Distinct error URI count (unique URLs returning 4xx/5xx)

📡 DATASOURCE: NewRelic/Infinity (efbmrprl96ry8e) | NRQL: uniqueCount(capture(uri)) WHERE 4xx/5xx

📐 CALC: uniqueCount(capture(uri)) WHERE status 4xx/5xx from NR Log

🔧 TECHNICAL: Unique URL count. Green <5 (isolated), Yellow 5-20 (spreading), Red >20 (widespread). Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: High count = widespread issues across many endpoints (systemic). Low count = concentrated on a few URIs (specific bug). Guides investigation scope

url_options.data: {"query": "{ actor { account(id: 7463762) { nrql(query: \"SELECT uniqueCount(capture(message, r'.*(?:GET|POST|PUT|DELETE|HEAD) (?P<uri>/[^ ]+) .*')) AS 'URIs' FROM Log WHERE service = 'iis' AND message NOT LIKE '%#%' AND message NOT LIKE '%200 0%' AND message NOT LIKE '%304 0%' SINCE ${__from:date:seconds} UNTIL ${__to:date:seconds}\") { results } } } }"}

panels.title: Error URIs

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: Error status code timeline (temporal pattern of each error type)

📡 DATASOURCE: NewRelic/Infinity (efbmrprl96ry8e) | NRQL: filter(count(*), WHERE status=X) TIMESERIES

📐 CALC: NRQL with multiple filter() aggregations for each status code (400, 403, 404, 500, 502, 503). TIMESERIES 5 minutes

🔧 TECHNICAL: Per-status-code error count over time. 5-minute buckets. No fixed thresholds. Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: Shows temporal patterns of each error type. Correlate spikes with deployments, config changes, or upstream failures. 502 spike = upstream timeout

url_options.data: {"query": "{ actor { account(id: 7463762) { nrql(query: \"SELECT filter(count(*), WHERE message LIKE '% 400 %') AS '400', filter(count(*), WHERE message LIKE '% 403 %') AS '403', filter(count(*), WHERE message LIKE '% 404 %') AS '404', filter(count(*), WHERE message LIKE '% 500 %') AS '500', filter(count(*), WHERE message LIKE '% 502 %') AS '502', filter(count(*), WHERE message LIKE '% 503 %') AS '503' FROM Log WHERE service = 'iis' AND message NOT LIKE '%#%' TIMESERIES 5 minutes SINCE ${__from:date:seconds} UNTIL ${__to:date:seconds}\") { results } } } }"}

panels.title: Error Status Codes Timeline

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: 4xx/5xx error distribution by host (per-PP-server error count)

📐 CALC: 7 separate NRQL queries (one per PP01-PP07), each COUNT(*) WHERE 4xx/5xx AND hostname=PPxx. TIMESERIES 5 minutes

🔧 TECHNICAL: Per-host error counts over time. No fixed thresholds. One host with high errors = server-specific issue. Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: Isolates whether errors are server-specific or fleet-wide. One host dominating = check that server specifically. Even distribution = systemic issue

url_options.data: {"query": "{ actor { account(id: 7463762) { nrql(query: \"SELECT filter(count(*), WHERE message LIKE '% 404 %' OR message LIKE '% 403 %' OR message LIKE '% 500 %' OR message LIKE '% 502 %' OR message LIKE '% 503 %') AS 'Errors' FROM Log WHERE service = 'iis' AND hostname = 'iNMANPR-PP01' TIMESERIES 5 minutes SINCE ${__from:date:seconds} UNTIL ${__to:date:seconds}\") { results } } } }"}

panels.title: 4xx/5xx Errors by Host

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: Error rate vs previous day (percentage comparison with yesterday)

📡 DATASOURCE: NewRelic/Infinity (efbmrprl96ry8e) | NRQL: percentage(count(*), WHERE error) COMPARE WITH 1 day ago

📐 CALC: percentage(count(*), WHERE error status) COMPARE WITH 1 day ago. NR COMPARE WITH creates overlay. TIMESERIES 5 minutes

🔧 TECHNICAL: Percentage comparison. Today vs yesterday overlay. Rising above yesterday = regression. Unit: percent

📊 BUSINESS: Shows whether errors are improving or degrading vs yesterday. Useful for detecting gradual regression after deployments

url_options.data: {"query": "{ actor { account(id: 7463762) { nrql(query: \"SELECT percentage(count(*), WHERE message NOT LIKE '%200 0%' AND message NOT LIKE '%304 0%') AS 'ErrorPct' FROM Log WHERE service = 'iis' AND message NOT LIKE '%#%' TIMESERIES 5 minutes SINCE ${__from:date:seconds} UNTIL ${__to:date:seconds} COMPARE WITH 1 day ago\") { results } } } }"}

panels.title: Error Rate vs Previous Day

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: 5xx errors vs previous day (absolute count comparison with yesterday)

📡 DATASOURCE: NewRelic/Infinity (efbmrprl96ry8e) | NRQL: COUNT(*) WHERE 5xx COMPARE WITH 1 day ago

📐 CALC: COUNT(*) WHERE capture(status) LIKE '5%' COMPARE WITH 1 day ago. TIMESERIES 5 minutes

🔧 TECHNICAL: Absolute 5xx count comparison. More sensitive than error rate for detecting server-side regressions. Unit: short

📊 BUSINESS: More sensitive than error rate % for detecting 5xx regressions. Any 5xx spike higher than yesterday = investigate immediately

url_options.data: {"query": "{ actor { account(id: 7463762) { nrql(query: \"SELECT filter(count(*), WHERE message LIKE '% 500 %' OR message LIKE '% 502 %' OR message LIKE '% 503 %' OR message LIKE '% 504 %') AS '5xx' FROM Log WHERE service = 'iis' AND message NOT LIKE '%#%' TIMESERIES 5 minutes SINCE ${__from:date:seconds} UNTIL ${__to:date:seconds} COMPARE WITH 1 day ago\") { results } } } }"}

panels.title: 5xx Errors vs Previous Day

panels.description:
📋 SUMMARY: Error URL breakdown (every URL returning 4xx/5xx with status + hit count)

📡 DATASOURCE: NewRelic/Infinity (efbmrprl96ry8e) | NRQL: COUNT(*) FACET uri, status WHERE 4xx/5xx
